rulings.Case Title: JDF HOLDINGS, LLC ET AL VS. QBE INSURANCE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery calendar for Friday, January 31 , 2025 Line 1. 2 - DEFENDANT QBE INSURANCE CORPORATION's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. Continued to February 24, 2025. Opposing party is ordered to provide courtesy copies immediately in compliance with SF Local Rule 2.7B. Moving party is advised that it is allowed to lodge courtesy copies of the opposition. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. (302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: JDF HOLDINGS, LLC ET AL VS. QBE INSURANCE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery calendar for Friday, January 31 , 2025 Line 1. 1 - DEFENDANTs MARSH & MCLENNAN AGENCY LLC,and KEVIN MIRSKY's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. Defendants MARSH & MCLENNAN AGENCY LLC and KEVIN MIRSKY's ("Moving Defendants") motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative summary adjudication, which is unopposed, is GRANTED Plaintiffs state one cause of action-the Third Cause of Action-against Moving Defendants. Moving Defendants have carried their initial burden by producing evidence demonstrating that they satisfied the duty of care they owed to Plaintiffs. (SUF 1-26.) Plaintiffs presented no evidence that could support a finding otherwise. There is no evidence allowing a trier of fact to find Moving Defendants owed Plaintiffs a heightened duty. (See Murray v. UPS Cap. Ins. Agency, Inc. (2020) 54 Cal.App.5th 628, 639; SUF 10-12, 17, 23-26.) Additionally, Plaintiffs did not present a separate statement, and the court grants the motion on this independent grounds. (See CCP section 437c(b)(2).) Judgment shall be entered in favor of Moving Defendants and against all Plaintiffs as to the Third COA. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. (302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: MAHER MEMARZADEH VS. ANDREW NICHOLAS DIMITRIOU ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery calendar for Friday, January 31 , 2025 Line 4. DEFENDANTs ANDREW DIMITRIOU, and DIMITRIOU & ASSOC, PC DBA ERGON LAW GROUP DEMURRER to COMPLAINT. Defendants ANDREW DIMITRIOU and DIMITRIOU & ASSOC, PC's demurrer is OVERRULED. The issue on demurrer is, taking the facts properly pleaded and properly noticed as true, does the challenged cause of action necessarily fail to state a cause for relief. (See Code Civ. Proc., 430.10(e); Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318.) In assessing whether the complaint states a cause of action, the court accepts all properly pleaded material facts, but not contentions, deductions, or conclusions of fact or law. (Minton v. Dignity Health (2019), 39 Cal.App.5th 1155, 1161.) "[I]f, on consideration of all facts stated, it appears that plaintiff is entitled to any relief against defendant, the complaint will be held good, though facts may not be clearly stated, or may be intermingled with a statement of other facts irrelevant to cause of action shown, or though plaintiff may demand relief to which he is not entitled under facts alleged." (Augustine v. Trucco (1954) 124 Cal.App.2d 229, 236, quoting Matteson v. Wagoner (1905) 147 Cal. 739, 742.) Here, Defendants' omnibus argument that all causes of action in Plaintiff Maher Memarzadeh's complaint are time barred under CCP section 340.6 is clearly without merit. Assuming the facts pleaded in the complaint as true, Defendants have not shown that each and every claim is time-barred, such that Plaintiff states no claim upon which relief can be granted. Defendants elected not to conduct cause-of-action specific analysis and, thus, the court will not conduct a cause-of-action specific analysis. Defendants to answer within 20 days. Plaintiff's request for a stay is denied without prejudice to the request being properly presented to the court. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. (302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: **TRANSFERRED TO ORANGE COUNTY**JOSE SANDOVAL ET AL VS. UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery calendar for Friday, January 31 , 2025 Line 5. DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., RAISER, LLC, RAISER-CA, LLC.'s MOTION TO TRANSFER. Defendant Uber Technologies, Inc.'s motion to transfer venue to Orange County based upon the convenience of witnesses and ends of justice is GRANTED. (CCP 397.) The only reasonable inference from the record is that key witnesses reside in or around Orange County and it would be inconvenient for them to appear in San Francisco. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.)
